title: conioselinum chinense subsp. boreale
intro: Conioselinum chinense subsp. boreale, also known as Korean angelica, is a subspecies of the angelica plant native to East Asia, characterized by its tall stalks and umbrella-like leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which help to relieve pain and reduce fever. The health benefits of Korean angelica include improving digestion, reducing menstrual cramps, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular herb in herbal medicine. Korean angelica is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
